_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Transoral surgery (Anterior) and CV Junction (Posterior Stabilization)
clinical_indications: Sphincter disturbances, lower cranial nerve dysfunction, and respiratory distress
Myelopathy, respiratory failure, vertebral artery dissection, neurologic compromise, and rarely quadriplegia or death if left untreated

preauth.label: Clinical notes including indication of implant requirement, evaluation findings confirming the diagnosis
CT + MRI CVJ (Craniovertebral junction) + cervical spine - X ray

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure/implant, and planned line of treatment?
Did clinical presentation and imaging confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Evidence of Oral Malignancy present without justification
Evidence of active naso-oral bleeding present without justification
Clinical notes missing indication for implant requirement, evaluation findings, or diagnosis confirmation
CT + MRI CVJ (Craniovertebral junction) and/or cervical spine X-ray not submitted at pre-authorization
Planned line of treatment not provided at pre-authorization
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) not submitted at claim stage or lacking daily vitals/treatment details
Post-op CT-CVJ (Craniovertebral junction) report not submitted at claim stage
Detailed procedure / operative notes not submitted at claim stage
Implant details (barcode/invoice) not submitted at claim stage
Detailed discharge summary not submitted at claim stage or lacking follow-up advice

clinical_key_pointers: Atlantoaxial dislocation refers to a loss of stability between the atlas and axis (C1-C2), resulting in loss of normal articulation.
The atlantoaxial joints can lose stable articulation from traumatic, inflammatory, idiopathic, or congenital abnormalities.
The mechanism of injury typically remains unidentified with several theories proposed in the literature.
Although it occurs in all age groups, atlantoaxial dislocation is most often seen in adolescents.
The atlantoaxial dislocation can be a mobile and reducible type or a “fixed” or irreducible type. The dislocation can also be partially reducible.
surgical_options: Transorally released anteriorly before posterior fusion
